problem: How are long-term memories physically encoded in neural tissue?
mechanism: Fear memory reconsolidation interference via timing
hypothesis: How are long-term memories physically encoded in neural tissue? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Fear. Fear memory reconsolidation interference via timing directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: fMRI connectivity study with 32 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: We cannot solve problems with the same thinking that created them. — Einstein paraphrase
